title: NC (CC-BY-NC) acceptance policy for OER + downstream-commercial caveat
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. This policy governs when a NonCommercial-licensed OER may be mapped and how the commercial caveat propagates downstream.
objective: Author the acceptance policy for CC-BY-NC (and other NonCommercial) OER and the downstream-commercial caveat carried on derived records.
acceptanceCriteria: Documents when an NC-licensed OER may be mapped, recording permitsDerivatives and commercialUse=false from a cited clause + snapshot, consistent with gate-002.
States the downstream-commercial caveat on every record/export derived from an NC source so downstream reusers know the referenced OER forbids commercial reuse.
Clarifies that the crosswalk record's outputLicense (CC-BY-4.0) does not relicense the referenced OER: the OER keeps its NC terms; the mapping is the CC-BY artefact.
Cross-references gate-002 so the licence gate applies the NC policy consistently.
Output licensed CC-BY-4.0; the commit is DCO signed-off.
